frame_analyses.description: Title slide showing 'Suturing for FLS: a practical tutorial'
Objective slide outlining tutorial goals for FLS suturing tasks
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Introduction to extracorporeal and intracorporeal knot tying

frame_analyses.description: External demonstration showing proper needle grip technique outside trainer
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Instruction to grasp suture at stiffest part within 1cm of needle

frame_analyses.description: Demonstration of needle stabilization at point 2 inside FLS trainer
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Proper needle positioning and stabilization technique being shown

frame_analyses.description: Warning about not avulsing drain from block
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Critical safety instruction - drain avulsion results in automatic zero score

frame_analyses.description: Slide showing intracorporeal knot tying specifications
frame_analyses.technique_notes: 15cm suture, 10 minute max time, 112 second target proficiency time displayed

frame_analyses.description: Demonstration of right hand turning needle clockwise to make rainbow configuration
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Proper surgeon's knot technique - creating the double throw configuration

frame_analyses.description: Showing 2 clockwise wraps for surgeon's knot
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Double throw being demonstrated - this is the key requirement for surgeon's knot

frame_analyses.description: Smiley face direction of needle being highlighted
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Important visual cue for proper needle orientation during knot tying

frame_analyses.description: Pulling suture ends in opposite directions to cinch down knot
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Proper knot tightening technique - equal tension in opposite directions

frame_analyses.description: Demonstrating wrapping with small circles near instrument tips
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Second throw technique - single wrap as required for proper knot sequence

drain_assessment.assessment_note: No actual task performance observed - this is an educational tutorial

technique_summary: This is an educational tutorial video demonstrating proper FLS Task 5 intracorporeal suturing technique, not an actual task performance. The video shows excellent instructional content covering needle loading, surgeon's knot formation, and proper knot tying sequences.
improvement_suggestions: This is a tutorial, not a performance to be improved
